content: Instead of forcing single agents to handle long, complex reasoning tasks that exceed their working memory limits, we can decompose the full reasoning chain into a sequence of small, memory-bounded subtasks assigned to specialized agents in a multi-agent orchestrated pipeline, where each agent only needs to retain enough working memory for its own narrow subtask. This turns the hard constraint of individual agent working memory limits from a flaw into a design feature that improves overall pipeline predictability and reliability.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
